content.text:
Now I can clearly see the molecule with atom labels. Looking at the image, I can identify atom C:39 (carbon with index 39) in the center-right region of the molecule. 

Atom C:39 is connected to:
- N:34 (nitrogen, index 34) - visible above and to the left
- C:33 (carbon, index 33) - visible to the left
- C:35 (carbon, index 35) - visible to the right
- N:38 (nitrogen, index 38) - visible below

Let me verify these connections by examining the bonds more carefully. From the image, atom C:39 appears to be a central carbon atom with four bonds:
1. Bond to N:34 (nitrogen)
2. Bond to C:33 (carbon) 
3. Bond to C:35 (carbon)
4. Bond to N:38 (nitrogen)

All these atoms are directly connected to C:39 via covalent bonds.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 6182, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_6182.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 39 (C) are:
- Atom index 34, element N
- Atom index 33, element C  
- Atom index 35, element C
- Atom index 38, element N

Geomconf_index: 6182

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 39, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
